paragraphs.body: How to tame a Philly Tongue

Philadelphia PA, where the crime rate is at an all-time high, drugs plague the streets, violence, and guns influence the youth, and a place where not too many opportunities present themselves that offer the people of Philly hopes of a better life. It wasn’t always this way though, there was a time when the parks were full, the streets were safe for kids to play in and there was a sense of hope and joy in the communities.

To live in Philly means knowing there are not too many opportunities that present themselves. Especially when you aren’t looking for a way out. You must work your way to the top just like everyone else, but it’ll be harder because of where you come from and how you believe your path to be. You might hit bumps in the road from people who are given every opportunity to be the person they want to be. That’s why you take every opportunity that presents itself As you get older through cities like Philadelphia you start to notice there aren’t enough resources or opportunities for hope to be instilled in the youth or even adults. My family had a saying for me, my sibling, and my cousin as we grew up, we heard “You Get What You Deserve” This saying lives through me every day as it is the reason I'm who I am. One time I didn’t do so well in school, I was getting in trouble, bad grades, the typical bad kid stuff, and when it came time for report cards, my uncle asked to see me and my brothers( he’s 1 year older than me) and he looks so disappointed at me but when he saw my brothers he praised him which is a valid thing to do because he had good grades and I didn’t he ended up taking him to get shoes and he made me sit there and watch while he got some and I didn’t, out of anger I said to him “that’s not fair” and he responded with You Get What You Deserve!

annotations.text: You might hit bumps in the road from people who are given every opportunity to be the person they want to be. That’s why you take every opportunity that presents itself

As you get older through cities like Philadelphia you start to notice there aren’t enough resources or opportunities for hope to be instilled in the youth or even adults. My family had a saying for me, my sibling, and my cousin as we grew up, we heard “You Get What You Deserve”
annotations.comment: It's so interesting that you're connecting the lack of opportunity and resources for Philly people with this phrase. Do you think people in Philly don't deserve better? Do you think other people agree? Is it true? Where does this idea come from?
Similarly, do you think that the lack of opportunity leads people to poor decisions? Do you think lack of opportunity makes people think less of themselves?

paragraphs.body: What this saying means is that to get the blessing you are so hopeful for and the things you so desperately want you must work for them. Granted opportunities are not presented often but when they are it is your job and your responsibility to make the most of them, especially as an adult. But for kids and high Highschoolers that’s a whole other conversation because due to the crime and violence that surround the youth, they aren’t aware of most things that present themselves whether it’s because of money, living situations, or just because there aren’t as many people as possible who care to show the youth their ways out and ways to get where they want to be.

annotations.text: But for kids and high Highschoolers that’s a whole other conversation because due to the crime and violence that surround the youth, they aren’t aware of most things that present themselves whether it’s because of money, living situations, or just because there aren’t as many people as possible who care to show the youth their ways out and ways to get where they want to be.
annotations.comment: Interesting context. I wonder if you should write about this idea (which repeats an idea you talked about in your first paragraph) with more concrete and specific detail?

paragraphs.body: For me, Hearing the saying You get what you deserve so much, developed me into a person who looks for ways to get ahead and find opportunities I wouldn’t be able to find just going to school and going back home. There are two ways people see themselves getting out of Philly and that is either Sports or School, But what happens when someone doesn’t have it in them to move to the next level of the options, I’m grateful that I was able to move to the next level for my education hoping for a career in something greater for my future but some don’t aren’t as lucky and that’s a message I want to spread. There are other ways to get ahead in life instead of school and sports but because there are so few voices in Black communities, not just Philly that show and share other ways to get what they deserve they slowly lose hope and start to think that they don’t deserve anything more than what Philadelphia has to offer which leads to violence, drug dealing, less graduate. All of this is because they feel like it isn’t worth it.

paragraphs.body: But Everyone deserves opportunities, people deserve to know there are other ways to what they deserve. Hearing that saying all my life is what built my work ethic and my drive to get ahead I think everyone deserves to have that type of support and system that shows them that there are ways and other things the world has to offer, other than being limited to what other social classes want us to see and never obtain.

Philly A low-income city with little to no hope left, just looking for voices that lead to a spark in faith. It is portrayed as a place filled with violence and wild-like communities but realistically, it’s just a place looking for more opportunities to show that there’s more to the city then just what is portrayed
